cards.front: What is data in machine learning?
cards.back: Data in machine learning refers to the input information used to train, test, and evaluate machine learning models.
The Big Data Analysis Framework consists of the Hadoop ecosystem, including HDFS, MapReduce, and Pig, along with tools like Spark and Hive.
Descriptive Statistics involves summarizing and describing the basic features of data, such as mean, median, mode, and standard deviation.
Univariate Data Analysis and Visualization involves examining and representing one variable at a time, often using plots like histograms and box plots.
Univariate Data Analysis involves analyzing and visualizing one variable at a time to understand its distribution, central tendency, and variability.
